rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO MONTEREY COUNTY ** G DANIEL WALKER VS. SCOTT KERNAN ET AL Provide Access
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Transfer Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANTS SCOTT KERNAN, MICHAEL STAINER, N COLVIN, S MIRANDA, K GREEN, W. MUNIZ'S Motion To Transfer Action Defendants' motion to transfer venue to Monterey County is granted. This is a prisoner's civil action alleging denial of access to the prison law library and the court system while plaintiff was incarcerated at Salinas Valley State Prison in Soledad, California, which is located in Monterey County. The proper county in which to bring suit against a public officer is the county in which the cause, or some part of the cause, arose, which in this case is Monterey County. (Code Civ. Proc. § 393(b).) The Court understands that Mr. Walker intends to appear telephonically at the hearing and has ordered administrators of the prison facility where he is housed to make the necessary arrangements to facilitate that appearance. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: HECTOR GANDHI VS. WELLS FARGO & CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Against Defendants Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. And Wells Fargo Equipment Finance, Inc., To Enforce Or Alternatively Reopen Discovery To Bring A Motion To Compel Further Document Responses. (Code Of Civil Procedure Section 2024.050(A)), Memorandum In Support, Declaration Of Marjorie A. Wallace In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday November 14, 2019 Line 1 - PLAINTIFF HECTOR GANDHI's Motion Against Defendants Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. And Wells Fargo Equipment Finance, Inc., To Enforce Or Alternatively Reopen Discovery To Bring A Motion To Compel Further Document Responses. Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Judge Pro Tem with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(JPT/302)

rulings.Case Title: MOSAICA 601 HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION A CALIFORNIA VS. CALIFORNIA HOMESTEAD ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant California Homestead Association'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2019, Line 14, Defendant California Homestead Association's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendants California Homestead Association and James E. Roberts-Obayashi Corporation's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Plaintiff's first cause of action is denied. A motion for judgment on the pleadings lies only for defects on the face of the complaint-extrinsic matters are improper. (Code Civ. Proc., § 438(d).) As such, the Court cannot consider discovery admissions or other evidence controverting the pleadings. (Id.; see Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2018) 7:322.) Defendants' heavy reliance on such extrinsic evidence, including the expert report referred to in its moving papers and the numerous documents as to which it seeks judicial notice, is improper. Defendants' requests for judicial notice are denied, and Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the attached documents are sustained. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to state a claim for a violation of the Right to Repair Act. (Civ. Code § 895 et seq.) The complaint alleges violations of Civil Code §§ 896, subd. (a)(14), subd. (a)(15), and subd. (g)(3)(A). (See Compl. 37.) Plaintiff does not solely seek recovery for an alleged defect in a manufactured product. (Civ. Code, § 896(g)(3)(E); cf. Kohler Co. v. Superior Court (2018) Cal.App.5th 55, 63 ["A homeowner alleging that a manufactured product-such as a plumbing fixture-installed in her home is defective may bring a claim under the Act only if the allegedly defective product caused a violation of one of the standards set forth in section 896."]) Plaintiff's claims are not barred by the statute of limitations. (See Civ. Code, § 941, subd. (a) ["Subject to certain exceptions, no action may be brought under Civil Code § 895 et seq. more than 10 years after substantial completion of the development, but not later than 10 years after recordation of a valid notice of completion."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. The court will sign the proposed order included in the moving papers. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER LAPPING VS. WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT WYNDHAM VACATION OWNERSHIP'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant Derek Milholland's motion to set aside entry of the default entered against him on August 12, 2019 is granted. Mr. Milholland claims that he was never personally served with the summons and complaint, and that the papers were left with his wife. (Milholland Decl. 3-4.) The registered process server in Hawaii who signed the proof of personal service says that he personally served Mr. Milholland after confirming his identity by name. (West Decl. 4.) There is reason to doubt Mr. Milholland's (or his counsel's) veracity. His counsel represented to Plaintiff's counsel that it was "impossible" that Mr. Milholland could have been personally served because he "did not live at the address listed on the proof of service form" (Higginbotham Decl. 14 & Ex. C), but Mr. Milholland squarely contradicts that assertion in his own declaration. (See Milholland Decl. 3.) However, the Court cannot definitively determine one way or the other whether Mr. Milholland was, in fact, properly served. The motion must be granted due to California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, Plaintiff's counsel's failure to notify Mr. Milholland or his counsel in advance of his intention to take the default, and the lack of any legal prejudice to Plaintiff Christopher Lapping by the granting of the motion. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 ["Because the law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits, any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default."].) Mr. Milholland must file his answer or other responsive pleading within 10 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Milholland is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default; Exhibit A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 14, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC., U.S. BANK N.A.'S Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default Defendant Specialized Loan Servicing LLC's motion to vacate the entry of default taken against it on August 23, 2019 is granted, and the hearing on Plaintiff's motion for entry of default judgment set for December 10, 2019 in Dept. 514 is vacated. Defendant has shown that default was entered due to its mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect due to its failure to promptly assign the case to outside counsel as a result of short staffing and miscommunications in its legal department. (Sullivan Decl. 3-5, 7.) Further, the Court finds that Plaintiff's counsel acted unprofessionally by taking Defendant's default only one day after the deadline for it to file a responsive pleading, without providing any advance notice to Defendant. (See Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135.) Finally, Plaintiff has not shown that it will suffer any prejudice if the motion is granted. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 [where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default."].) Accordingly, the motion is granted. Defendant shall file its answer or other responsive pleading within 10 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
